Saturday Summary: Wootton Bassett adds a new Classic winner

Super sire Extreme Choice added to his already incredible record with G2 BRC Sires’ Produce Stakes winner Berzelius. He’ll head to the G1 JJ Atkins Plate where he’ll be joined by Rosehill winner Tron Bolt.

Stakes action continues across four states on Saturday, but the headliners are at Eagle Farm, where Robert Wellwood hopes Solid Gold can break the fillies' 16-year drought in the G1 Queensland Derby and Peter Tighe hopes Silken Salute puts his hand up for a G1 JJ Atkins Plate tilt.

Peter V’landys’ temporary move to the NRL, Graeme Hinton’s elevation as interim CEO, the looming Hazzard review and Racing NSW’s Court of Appeal battle with the ATC all landed in the same five-day stretch. TTR runs through the week at Racing NSW - and what it could mean for the governance, leadership and direction of the state’s racing industry.

When Silverdale secured $750,000 stakes winner Liquor at Magic Millions, it was more than the purchase of one mare. For Farm Manager Rob Petith, it brought together an old connection, a fast-rising broodmare band, and the story of how Steve Grant’s Southern Highlands operation has built itself into one of the most ambitious young farms in the country.

Magic Millions offered fewer weanlings and turned over less money in 2026, but the real story sits in the quartiles: a cooled top end, a firmer floor, and a physical marketplace being reshaped by cost, caution and digital alternatives.

The Inglis Digital May (Late) Sale concluded on Thursday, and the 276 lots sold was highlighted by multiple Grade I winner In Italian's half-sister Fiorenza, who was purchased by Arrowfield Stud, and Group 2-winning West Of Swindon, who was bought by Pure Bloodstock.

Stiff competition at all levels resulted in lifts to both the median and the clearance rate at the end of the second day of the 2026 Magic Millions Gold Coast National Broodmare Sale. Valuable pregnancies to Extreme Choice were the stars of the show, with Yulong going to $2.05 million to secure young Hellbent mare Miss Hellfire in foal to him on her maiden cover.

The top end of the market remained strong during the broodmare session of the Magic Millions Gold Coast National Broodmare Sale. While the number of million-dollar mares was significantly down on last year, competition for the most sought-after offerings remained strong.
